articles.title: ‘Rogue U.S Cities & States Not Cooperating’: Rep. Arrington DESTROYS House Dems In Roaring Testimony
articles.description: A fiery debate over immigration enforcement erupted on Capitol Hill as Republican Representative Jodey Arrington defended increased funding for border security and ICE operations while criticizing Democratic opposition. During a heated hearing, Arrington argued that some cities and states were failing to cooperate with federal immigration authorities, describing the situation as a major obstacle to enforcing U.S. immigration laws. The testimony quickly became one of the most contentious moments of the hearing, drawing strong reactions from both sides. Watch.

articles.title: US lawmakers cheer as court blocks $100,000 H-1B fee in major blow to Trump visa curbs
articles.description: US lawmakers welcomed a court ruling blocking a proposed USD 100,000 H-1B visa fee, despite White House plans to appeal. The decision crucially protects the vital employment pipeline for highly skilled Indian professionals, who systematically secure the majority of these 85,000 annual US work permits.

articles.title: Air Force sergeant held for attempting to murder woman over suspicion of infidelity
articles.description: An IAF sergeant, Sachin Mandhve, was arrested for allegedly attempting to murder a woman during an argument over her character at a hospital parking lot.

articles.title: “BTS works in an organic and fluid way": Football fans make BTS’ “Dynamite” the unofficial anthem of the FIFA World Cup online
articles.description: BTS’ hit song “Dynamite” has unexpectedly become part of a viral FIFA World Cup trend on TikTok and X. Football fans are using the track in South Korea-themed match edits, with several clips reportedly crossing 500,000 likes. The trend sparked hilarious reactions from ARMY members, many surprised that even non-K-pop fans know the song. Social media users say the crossover feels natural, proving BTS’ global cultural influence continues to stretch far beyond music and into sports culture.

articles.title: Cristiano Ronaldo’s reported breakup deal with Georgina Rodriguez could cost him over $114,000 every month
articles.description: Cristiano Ronaldo and Georgina Rodriguez’s engagement announcement has sparked fresh attention around their reported financial agreement. Reports claim that if the couple ever separates, Rodriguez could receive more than $114,000 every month for life, along with ownership of Ronaldo’s Madrid mansion. The arrangement reportedly protects Ronaldo’s billion-dollar fortune while ensuring long-term financial security for Rodriguez and their children. The story has quickly become one of the most talked-about celebrity and football headlines online.

articles.title: “33 points non negotiables list”: Morgan Riddle drops surprising Taylor Fritz breakup stance on dating athletes again
articles.description: Morgan Riddle revealed in an interview with Elle that she and tennis star Taylor Fritz ended their six-year relationship around two months ago. While saying they remain on good terms, Riddle also stated she will “never date an athlete again.” She emphasized the importance of financial independence, personal growth, and building her own career as she begins a new chapter in New York.

articles.title: ‘Kristi Noem Stole Billions’: Trump 'Corruption' Bombshell Rocks Cong During ICE Hearing | US House
articles.description: Democratic Representative Mary Gay Scanlon used a contentious congressional hearing on a proposed $70 billion funding increase for U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) to raise concerns about accountability within the Department of Homeland Security (DHS). Arguing against the funding package, Scanlon alleged widespread corruption within the department and accused former DHS Secretary Kristi Noem and her associates of involvement in misconduct worth billions of dollars. The claims intensified an already heated debate over immigration enforcement, government oversight, and federal spending, as lawmakers sparred over whether ICE should receive a major expansion of resources and authority.

articles.title: Oil tanker catches fire near Oman; 24 Indian seafarers safe
articles.description: An empty oil tanker, MT Marivex, caught fire near Oman on Monday. All 24 Indian seafarers on board are safe. The vessel was outside the Strait of Hormuz when the incident occurred. The cause of the fire is under investigation. This event happened amidst heightened tensions between Iran and Israel, which saw exchanges of attacks.

articles.title: Philippines Earthquake: CCTV Footage Of The Moment Tremor Hit Davao City
articles.description: A powerful 7.8-magnitude earthquake struck offshore near General Santos in the southern Philippines early Monday, causing significant damage and triggering a tsunami. The quake damaged buildings and a key access bridge in Davao, one of the region’s largest cities, while videos on social media showed shops shaking and people fleeing in panic. The disaster claimed at least four lives and injured more than 200 people. A tsunami measuring about 1 meter (3 feet) washed ashore along nearby coastlines, prompting authorities to issue warnings and evacuation advisories.

articles.title: 'US Won't Hesitate...': Trump's Final Warning? Iran Faces Uranium Red Line As Deal Hangs In Balance
articles.description: Donald Trump has issued a stark message to Tehran, declaring that Iran’s highly enriched uranium will be destroyed either through a negotiated deal or through military action. In a revealing interview, Trump claimed Washington and Tehran are close to an agreement but warned that the U.S. would act forcefully if talks collapse. He also signaled openness to engaging with Iran’s new leadership while insisting that Tehran must never be allowed to acquire nuclear weapons. The comments offer a rare glimpse into the high-stakes diplomacy shaping the future of the Iran conflict.
